chart.title: Share of population living in urban areas
chart.subtitle: Share of the population living in urban areas, based on each country's national definition.
chart.note: Cross-country comparability is limited because of differences in national definitions.
chart.citation: United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Population Division (2025)

Share of the population living in urban.titleShort: Share of population living in urban areas
Share of the population living in urban.titleLong: Share of population living in urban areas - UN national definitions
Share of the population living in urban.descriptionShort: Share of the population living in urban areas, based on each country's national definition.
descriptionKey: Countries classify areas as urban or rural using their own criteria, such as administrative boundaries, population size or density, economic activity, or some combination of these. As a result, similar settlements can be classified differently across countries.

Definitions can also change within a country over time, creating breaks in the series that reflect reclassification rather than real changes in where people live. For cross-country comparisons that avoid these issues, we also publish indicators based on the [Degree of Urbanization (DEGURBA)](https://human-settlement.emergency.copernicus.eu/degurbaDefinitions.php), a harmonized framework that classifies settlements by population density and size using consistent criteria across countries. You can find [these indicators here](https://ourworldindata.org/search?datasetProducts=Global+Human+Settlement+Layer+Dataset).
Share of the population living in urban.citationShort: United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs, Population Division (2025) – with minor processing by Our World in Data
